cumulative_incidence.note: Aalen-Johansen discrete-time cumulative incidence over the panel; mergers are ~4x more common than failures, which is why treating them as a competing risk matters.

informative_censoring.interpretation: 81 of 3718 merger-exit banks (2.2%) were at or above the review threshold at their last filing - would-be positives removed from the labelable pool by censoring, biasing measured failure recall downward. This bounds the informative-censoring bug: the true recall is at most this fraction higher than measured.

method: cause-specific hazards (failure + merger) + Aalen-Johansen CIF, implemented from scratch (no new deps, $0). A full Fine-Gray subdistribution model has since been BUILT (ml/scripts/fine_gray.py, within noise of cause-specific); this quantifies the bias the current censoring introduces, which is the decision-relevant number.
